nodes.label: i | wish | ∅
nodes.sentence: I wished to join their circle, to walk alongside them and share their path, but they would not permit it.

nodes.label: i | speak | they
nodes.sentence: I spoke to them earnestly: "Among proud warriors and wise elders, it is not the way to turn a face from those who walk humbly, nor to deny oneself the strength found in such company.

nodes.label: i | carry | blanket
nodes.sentence: Though I lack a horse, my spirit is strong—I can carry our blankets and ease your load."

nodes.label: one | reply | ∅
nodes.sentence: Yet one among them replied, "Do not be offended by our words.

nodes.label: brother | come | ∅
nodes.sentence:
Not long ago, a false brother, cloaked like a medicine man, came and joined us, but his heart was dark."

How can one truly know the spirit beneath a painted face?

nodes.label: storyteller | know | truth
nodes.sentence: The storyteller knows the truth the tale must hold.

nodes.label: they | welcome | he
nodes.sentence: Because the medicine men are trusted, they welcomed him with open hands and hearts, believing him a friend.

nodes.label: man | wear | garment
nodes.sentence: The true wise men wear humble garments of patched leather and soft cloth, showing only their honest selves to others.

nodes.label: ∅ | let | ∅
nodes.sentence: Let deeds be your adornment, no matter the robe you wear.

nodes.label: ∅ | mean | ∅
nodes.sentence: To walk the sacred path means to empty one’s heart of greed and desire—not merely to cast aside outer garments.

nodes.label: strength | show | itself
nodes.sentence: True strength shows itself in the battles of life.

nodes.label: weapon | be | good
nodes.sentence: What good are weapons to one who cannot face the fight with courage?

nodes.label: we | make | camp
nodes.sentence: One night, as we traveled under the quiet gaze of the moon, we made camp near a watchful stone fort.

nodes.label: brother | slip | shadow
nodes.sentence: The false brother, lifting a water gourd from a companion as though to cleanse his hands in ritual, slipped away into the shadows to steal.

nodes.label: pretender | turn | feather
nodes.sentence:
A pretender who wears the healer’s mantle 
Turns the sacred eagle feathers into mere tassels for an enemy’s horse.

nodes.label: he | vanish | sight
nodes.sentence: He vanished from our sight and climbed a tall watchtower, where he took a box of precious things.

nodes.label: stranger | go | dawn
nodes.sentence: By dawn, the black-hearted stranger had gone far along the trail.

nodes.label: innocent | seize | ∅
nodes.sentence: When morning came, the innocent, still asleep in camp, were seized and cast into the cold shadow of the fort.

nodes.label: we | turn | day
nodes.sentence: From that day forward, we turned from fellowship to solitude, living by this wisdom: safety lies in walking alone.

nodes.label: shame | drape | great
nodes.sentence:
When one soul in a tribe acts in dishonor, 
The shame drapes over both the great and the small.

nodes.label: you | see | taint
nodes.sentence:
Have you not seen how one wild horse 
Taints the spirit of the entire herd?

nodes.label: i | answer | they
nodes.sentence: I answered them with gratitude: “Great is the Spirit of the Earth and Sky!

nodes.label: i | bar | blessing
nodes.sentence: Though I walk apart from the medicine men, I have not been barred from their blessings.

nodes.label: story | reach | heart
nodes.sentence: Your story has reached my heart and will guide me through all my days.”

nodes.label: heart | carry | sorrow
nodes.sentence:
For one brash wolf among the pack, 
The hearts of wise elders carry a heavy sorrow.

nodes.label: diving | muddy | it
nodes.sentence:
If a river runs sweet with clear water, 
A wolf diving in can muddy it all.
